title: xyris involucrata
intro: Xyris involucrata, also known as the goldeneye or yellow-eyed grass, is a type of flowering plant native to Australia and Southeast Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Xyris involucrata include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, as well as its use as an expectorant to relieve coughs and colds. Xyris involucrata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
